zh.lifeSummary: Richard Rogers是John Rogers和Agnes Partey的兒子，於1551年6月出生在英國。他於大約1572年與Barbara Chandler在英國艾可維瑟斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1618年4月21日享年66歲，埋葬在英國艾可維瑟斯費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Rogers是John Rogers和Agnes Partey的儿子，于1551年6月出生在英国。他于大约1572年与Barbara Chandler在英国艾可維瑟斯費爾德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1618年4月21日享年66岁，埋葬在英国艾可維瑟斯費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Rogers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Roger , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Ruaidhrí (see Rorie ).

History: Thomas Rogers (c. 1587–1621), born in London, England, was among the Pilgrim Fathers who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620. He died during the first winter at Plymouth Colony, but his son Joseph survived and married, and was later joined in MA by his brother John. This name was subsequently brought to North America independently by many different bearers.

variants.name: Roger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Rodgers
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Rogers  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Richard Rogers was born in June 1551, in England as the son of John Rogers and Agnes Partey. He married Barbara Chandler about 1572, in Wethersfield, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 21 April 1618, at the age of 66, and was buried in Wethersfield, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Rogersは1551年6月にイギリスでJohn RogersとAgnes Parteyの息子として生まれた。約1572年にイギリスエセックスウエスエルスフィールドでBarbara Chandlerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1618年4月21日に66歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスウエスエルスフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Rogers(은)는 1551년 6월 영국에서 John Rogers(와)과 Agnes Partey의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1572년 영국 에세스 w더스필드에서 Barbara Chandler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1618년 4월 21일 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 w더스필드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Rogers родился июнь 1551 г. в местности Англия. Его родителей звали John Rogers и Agnes Partey. Он женился на женщине по имени Barbara Chandler около 1572 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 21 апреля 1618 г. в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности ветирсфиелд, эссекс, Англия.
